articles.title: US military ships enter Hormuz. Trump says they're cleaning out the mines
articles.expand: The talks in Islamabad have beguin, according to reports from all sides. Notably, Iran has said it can't open Hormuz because of mines in the water. The US seems to be calling that bluff as 'several' US navy ships passed through. Trump said "We're now starting the process of clearing out Hormuz". articles.title: UK to host more talks on reopening Strait of Hormuz shipping route
articles.expand: Britain will host further talks on reopening the vital Strait of Hormuz shipping lane with a coalition of countries next week. The meeting will continue the Government’s efforts to restore freedom of navigation to the strait, which provides shipping routes for oil and gas. It comes after the Prime Minister spoke to Donald Trump about the need for a...

articles.title: Former Fed official argues against Fed independence
articles.expand: Former Federal Reserve Vice Chair for Supervision Randal Quarles, who served during President Trump's first term, said members of the Fed board should be removable by the president, but that the decentralized structure of the Fed will ensure that monetary policy decisions remain sound.

articles.title: Fed says oil shock means getting inflation down takes longer
articles.expand: San Francisco Federal Reserve President Mary Daly said the US economy is fundamentally solid, the labour market has steadied, and monetary policy is in a “good place” — restrictive enough to put downward pressure on inflation without undercutting the labour market. articles.expand: Gold (XAU/USD) price remains steady on Friday, poised to finish the week with gains of almost 2% as the US Dollar tumbles amid optimism about Iran-US talks in Pakistan throughout the weekend. Also, a mildly high US inflation report trimmed the Federal Reserve's (Fed) dovish bets for 2026.

articles.title: NBP expands gold reserves and moves closer to the global Top 10
articles.expand: The NBP increased its gold reserves in March 2026 by about 13 tons, taking advantage of a sharp price correction. Total holdings rose to around 580 tons. Despite a lower valuation due to falling prices, the central bank continues its strategy of accumulation and is now just over 30 tons away from entering the global top 10 in gold reserves.
